latest.title: Trading Statement
latest.content:
18 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0438X
Dianomi PLC
18 March 2026
18 March 2026
Dianomi plc
("Dianomi", the "Company" or the "Group")
Trading and Publisher Expansion Update
Revenues In-Line and Improved Gross Margin Performance
Return to growth and profitability in H2 25
Dianomi, a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors, is pleased to provide an update on trading for the 12 months to 31 December 2025 ("FY25").
The Group expects to deliver revenues of £27.4 million (FY24: £28.0 million), in line with market expectations
*
. Strongly improved gross margin performance led to a year on year increase in gross profit to £7.5 million (FY24: £7.3 million), and an improved EBITDA loss of approximately £0.3 million (FY24: £0.3 million), substantially ahead of market forecasts*. Cash at year end was £5.8 million (FY24: £8.8 million) reflecting a return to profitability in the second half of the year. All FY25 figures are subject to audit.
While trading was held back by a subdued market reflecting broader macroeconomic conditions, Dianomi improved its margin performance through ongoing operational optimisations. The Group also expanded its product offering and continues to offer a very compelling proposition, underpinned by excellent distribution capability, reaching 500 million digital devices each month across a network of more than 250 premium publishers.
Looking forward, following successful trials and subsequent onboarding in FY24, post year end Dianomi has expanded its partnerships with both CNN News and Associated Press, adding additional units on new pages to its existing footprint across both publishers' wider sites, highlighting the attractiveness of Dianomi's solution to its publishing partners. The benefits from the expansion with both CNN News and Associated Press are expected to flow from Q2 FY26 onwards.
Furthermore, reflecting the focus on new product development and AI, a new partnership with AI media infrastructure company Dappier, was announced earlier this week, to launch an advertising monetised AI-powered financial answers engine designed for premium publisher websites.
By combining Dappier's conversational AI and monetization technology with Dianomi's scaled distribution across premium finance publishers and its financial advertising marketplace, the aim is to build a distributed AI answers network across the sector.
The Company looks forward to announcing its FY25 results in May 2026.
\* The Company believes market expectations for FY 2025 to be for revenues of £27.5 million, gross profit of £7.0 million and EBITDA loss of £1.1 million
Rupert Hodson, CEO of Dianomi, said:
"2025 saw a subdued market in which we traded well in the second half of the year following a challenging first half, returning to both growth and profitability. During the year we focused on investing in and developing our capabilities and our products. I believe the growth in our partnerships with CNN and Associated Press clearly demonstrate the value of the Dianomi proposition at the premium end of the market and the trust our partners place in our platform. Our focus is to fully leverage this opportunity. 2026 has seen some positive developments and we look forward to building on this momentum as the year progresses."
For further information contact:
Dianomi
Tel: +44 (0)207 802 5530
Rupert Hodson (Chief Executive Officer)
Charlotte Stranner (Chief Financial Officer)
Panmure Liberum (NOMAD and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)207 886 2500
Emma Earl, Corporate Finance
Rupert Dearden, Corporate Broking
Novella Communications
Tel: +44 (0)203 151 7008
Tim Robertson / Aeliya Birgrami
dianomiplc@novella-comms.com
About Dianomi
Dianomi, established in 2003, is a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors. The Group operates from its offices in London, New York and Sydney. The Group enables premium brands to deliver advertisements to a targeted audience on the desktop and mobile websites, mobile and tablet applications of premium publishers. It provides premium advertisers, including blue chip names such as Charles Schwab, Invesco and Bank of America, with access to an international audience of over 500 million devices per month through its partnerships with over 250 premium publishers, including blue chip names such as Reuters, CNN Business, the Times and WSJ. Adverts served are contextually relevant to the content of the webpages on which they appear and mirror the style of the page, which enhances reader engagement.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. latest.title: Partnership with AI Company Dappier
latest.content:
16 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7511W
Dianomi PLC
16 March 2026
16 March 2026
Dianomi plc
("Dianomi", the "Company" or the "Group")
New Partnership with AI Media Infrastructure Company Dappier
Dianomi, a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors, is pleased to announce it has formed a new partnership with AI media infrastructure company Dappier to launch an advertising monetised AI-powered financial answers engine designed for financial publisher websites, many of whom are existing clients of Dianomi.
Publishers of financial news websites are increasingly competing with alternatives such as AI news summaries and conversational AI chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude, the combination of which is reducing audience time on their sites.
Dianomi and Dappier's AI-powered answers engine combats this issue, by enabling publishers to turn their journalism and extensive archives into content for conversational AI experiences embedded directly on their own sites, enabling readers to ask questions that occur to them as shown in the mobile format example opposite.
Answers are generated from trusted editorial content and critically, delivered within the publisher environment. Doing so retains audiences and enables the AI engine to capture the questions being asked and integrate agentic native advertising directly into the conversational experience, creating a new revenue opportunity for both publishers and financial brands.
For publishers this represents an excellent opportunity to capture and monetise financial AI conversations directly on their own sites rather than losing audiences to external AI platforms and provides Dianomi with new agentic advertising opportunities within its existing publisher base and creates new ones with publishers with whom Dianomi does not currently partner.
By combining Dappier's conversational AI technology with Dianomi's distribution, which reaches 500 million devices per month across 250+ premium publishers, the partnership has the potential to create a new sector-wide distributed AI answers network.
Commenting on the partnership, Rupert Hodson, CEO of Dianomi, said:
"Financial decisions start with questions. AI is changing how those questions are asked, and we believe publishers should benefit from that shift, rather than lose audiences to external platforms.
Dianomi's role in this partnership is clear: we bring the premium financial advertisers and global distribution across the world's leading financial publishers. By leveraging Dappier's AI technology, we are transforming conversational financial intent into a scalable advertising opportunity thereby helping publishers to further monetise their sites and further embedding Dianomi within the publisher ecosystem.
While still in the early stages, the response has been very positive, and we are currently engaged in a number of discussions with major publishers and advertisers regarding potential pilot projects."
Dan Goikhman, CEO at Dappier, added:
"Our agentic AI technology enables publishers to turn their trusted content into real-time conversational answer experiences that provide a unique surface for brands to engage consumers. Partnering with Dianomi ensures those experiences are distributed at scale and monetised effectively through deep financial advertising relationships.
Together, we are creating a publisher-owned alternative to horizontal AI platforms - one purpose-built for the finance vertical."
For further information contact:
Dianomi
Tel: +44 (0)207 802 5530
Rupert Hodson (Chief Executive Officer)
Charlotte Stranner (Chief Financial Officer)
Panmure Liberum (NOMAD and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)207 886 2500
Emma Earl/ Freddy Crossley, Corporate Finance
Rupert Dearden, Corporate Broking
Novella Communications
Tel: +44 (0)203 151 7008
Tim Robertson / Aeliya Birgrami
dianomiplc@novella-comms.com
About Dianomi
Dianomi, established in 2003, is a leading provider of digital advertising services to premium clients in the Business, Finance and Lifestyle sectors. The Group operates from its offices in London, New York and Sydney. The Group enables premium brands to deliver advertisements to a targeted audience on the desktop and mobile websites, mobile and tablet applications of premium publishers. It provides premium advertisers, including blue chip names such as Charles Schwab, Invesco and Bank of America, with access to an international audience of over 500 million devices per month through its partnerships with over 250 premium publishers, including blue chip names such as Reuters, CNN Business, the Times and WSJ. Adverts served are contextually relevant to the content of the webpages on which they appear and mirror the style of the page, which enhances reader engagement.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: DNM Ltd builds and installs solar‑panel systems for residential and commercial customers, offering end‑to‑end solutions from design to maintenance.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The renewable‑energy market is expanding faster than fossil‑fuel alternatives, and DNM’s modular, low‑cost panels give it a competitive edge in price‑sensitive markets.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still ramping up production capacity, has recently secured a partnership with an AI firm to optimise panel placement, and is reporting a modest turnaround in cash flow. The share price sits near the lower end of its 52‑week range, indicating a steep drop from its peak.

rns_narrative: DNM announced a strategic alliance with an AI company to automate design and optimise energy yield, signalling a move toward higher‑margin, tech‑driven services.
The company delivered its latest half‑year results, showing a reduction in operating losses and a clearer path to profitability once the partnership matures.
Corporate governance changes – a new board chair and a shift in registered office – suggest a fresh start and a tighter focus on shareholder value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but hopeful – the community is largely silent, with a handful of bullish whispers about the AI tie‑up.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders note that the AI partnership could unlock a new revenue stream and that the company’s cost structure is becoming sustainable.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the firm’s history of missed targets, weak earnings, and a still‑unproven product line, warning that the partnership may not pay off quickly.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past year sentiment has slipped from mild optimism to near‑indifference; the market has largely ignored DNM’s announcements, creating a perfect contrarian window.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity – the stock is essentially invisible to the broader public.

near_term_catalysts: The AI partnership is expected to go live in Q3; a public release of the first pilot results could spark buying interest.
A scheduled earnings call next month will likely detail cost‑reduction progress – a positive surprise here would be a trigger.
If the share price breaks above the recent 20‑day moving average with volume exceeding 150% of the average, that would signal a reversal.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained uptick in institutional buying, a sudden jump in short‑interest reduction, and any mention of a new commercial contract that brings in a sizable upfront payment.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle – the price has fallen sharply, volume has tapered off, and the APEX score indicates a tight, compressed range. A mean‑reversion bounce is expected once the AI platform starts delivering results.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock rebounds to the midpoint of its pre‑crash range, the upside could easily double the current price – a 200% gain from the bottom. The deep crash (likely 70‑90% down) coupled with a new AI‑driven revenue engine gives a high‑reward, low‑probability scenario that fits the system’s proven colour profile.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The partnership’s success is unproven, and DNM still faces regulatory hurdles and a thin operating margin. A failure to secure a key commercial contract could keep the price stuck at the bottom, delivering a modest loss if the share is bought at the current trough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is capped at the current price (the worst that can happen is staying at the bottom). Potential upside is roughly double the entry level if the stock reaches the mid‑range, giving a risk‑to‑reward ratio of 1:2 or better.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the share closes above the 20‑day moving average on volume at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Allocate 10% of the portfolio to the position, set a stop 20% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the AI partnership announcement or the next earnings call. If either event shows a positive surprise, consider a small entry at 5% below the post‑announcement high to capture a quick bounce.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – no signs of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: DNM is a deep‑crashed play that could deliver a 200% upside once its AI partnership starts generating revenue, making it a prime contrarian opportunity.

panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: 📊 Moderate fear detected - monitor for escalation

fear_vs_facts.verdict: ⚖️ Mixed signals - Fear and facts balanced, wait for clarity

volume_death.description: High volume - Panic spike or interest

reasons_to_buy.reason: Proven winner - previously hit lock-in target

reasons_to_sell.reason: Round-trip collapse - pump and dump pattern
Stale signal - not for active trading

Historical data shows 1 rallies averaging 42% upside. Current position: -77.8%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
